case_title: FRANCISCO ROJAS ROLON VS. MENTOR TECHNICAL GROUP INTERNATIONAL ET AL

docket_entries.description: MINI MINUTES FOR JAN-21-26 09:00 AM FOR DEPT 302

DISCOVERY 302, 2 - DEFENDANT MENTOR TECHNICAL GROUP INTERNATIONAL LLC'S UNOPPOSED MOTION FOR A PROTECTIVE ORDER STAYING PLAINTIFF'S WRITTEN DISCOVERY PENDING DETERMINATION OF THE MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION IS DENIED AS MOOT. IN LIGHT OF THE COURT'S ORDER GRANTING DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY THE ACTION PENDING THE OUTCOME OF THE ARBITRATION, DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO STAY PLAINTIFF'S WRITTEN DISCOVERY PENDING DETERMINATION OF THE MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION IS DENIED AS MOOT. JUDGE: JOSEPH M. QUINN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (302/JMQ)
MINI MINUTES FOR JAN-23-26 09:00 AM FOR DEPT 302

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-11-2026 Continued To Sep-09-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION
Off Calendar Jan-23-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Mentor Technical Group International, L.L.C.'s Motion to Compel Arbitration is GRANTED.

(SEE WORD MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING)

Prevailing Party is ordered to prepare and submit a hard copy of a proposed order which repeats verbatim the Court's Ruling directly to Department 302.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn: Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/JMQ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For A Protective Order Staying Plaintiff'S Written Discovery Pending Determination Of The Motion To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Jan-23-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Mentor Technical Group International LLC's unopposed Motion For A Protective Order Staying Plaintiff's Written Discovery Pending Determination Of The Motion To Compel Arbitration is DENIED AS MOOT.

In light of the court's order granting Defendant's motion to compel arbitration and stay the action pending the outcome of the arbitration, Defendant's motion to stay Plaintiff's written discovery pending determination of the motion to compel arbitration is denied as moot.

Prevailing Party is ordered to prepare and submit a hard copy of a proposed order which repeats verbatim the Court's Ruling directly to Department 302.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn: Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/JMQ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For A Protective Order Staying Plaintiff'S Written Discovery Pending Determination Of The Motion To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Jan-21-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

2 - Defendant Mentor Technical Group International LLC's unopposed Motion For A Protective Order Staying Plaintiff's Written Discovery Pending Determination Of The Motion To Compel Arbitration is DENIED AS MOOT.

In light of the court's order granting Defendant's motion to compel arbitration and stay the action pending the outcome of the arbitration, Defendant's motion to stay Plaintiff's written discovery pending determination of the motion to compel arbitration is denied as moot.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION
Off Calendar Jan-21-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

1 - Defendant Mentor Technical Group International LLC's unopposed Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action is GRANTED.

Good cause appearing, Plaintiff Francisco Rojas Rolon is ordered to submit this dispute to arbitration under the Employee Acknowledgement and Agreement, executed by Plaintiff on February 15, 2019. (Vilanova-Medina Decl., ex. A.) The record demonstrates the parties entered an arbitration agreement and the agreement appears to cover the disputes at issue. Plaintiff has not established the agreement is unenforceable.

Plaintiff's claims against Defendant Mentor Technical Group International LLC are ordered to arbitration and the matter is stayed pending the outcome of the arbitration proceedings.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.
Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
